page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 21332.83 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 961.55 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, with Redis handling 362.1 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

The r8gn.48xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads due to its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and the power efficiency of the Graviton ARM architecture. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which should be factored into architectural designs. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by 192 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.7 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by its high Redis throughput of 362.1 million operations per second and 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. However, single-core performance remains average. Given its high memory density and efficient ARM architecture, the r8gn.48xlarge is highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and intensive multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 1536 GB of RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A high-density ARM64 memory-optimized instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and multi-core performance for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
